content:
🚀 mobile-mcp | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide a unified, platform-agnostic interface for automating native iOS and Android applications on various device types, enabling LLM-driven interactions and complex user journeys.
✨ Key Features: • Platform-agnostic interface for iOS and Android automation • Supports emulators, simulators, and real devices
📖 Summary: The Mobile MCP server acts as a platform-agnostic interface for automating and interacting with native iOS and Android applications. It allows agents and LLMs to control mobile devices (emulators, simulators, and real devices) using accessibility data or screenshot-based analysis, simplifying mobile development and automation tasks.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🌟 certimate | Go
🎯 Primary Use Case: Automating and simplifying the management of SSL certificates for self-hosted environments through a visual interface.
✨ Key Features: • Automated SSL certificate issuance, deployment, renewal, and monitoring • Self-hosted with full data control and privacy • Zero dependencies, single-click deployment • Low resource usage (~16MB memory) • User-friendly GUI for visual workflow orchestration
📖 Summary: Certimate is an open-source, self-hosted ACME tool that automates the entire lifecycle of SSL certificate management, including issuance, deployment, renewal, and monitoring. It offers a user-friendly GUI, zero dependencies, and low resource usage, making it suitable for various deployment scenarios.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 OpenMemory | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: To provide AI agents and LLM applications with persistent, long-term memory, enabling them to retain information and context across interactions without relying solely on stateless models or basic RAG.
✨ Key Features: • Real long-term memory for AI agents • Self-hosted, local-first storage (SQLite/Postgres) • Python and Node.js SDKs • Integrations with LangChain, CrewAI, AutoGen, Streamlit, MCP, VS Code
📖 Summary: OpenMemory is a self-hosted, local-first cognitive memory engine for AI agents, providing real long-term memory beyond simple embeddings. It offers SDKs for Python and Node.js, integrates with popular agent frameworks, and supports data ingestion from various sources.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

✨ sglang | Python
🎯 Primary Use Case: Serving large language models and multimodal models with high performance and low latency for inference.
✨ Key Features: • High-performance inference for LLMs and multimodal models • Optimized attention mechanisms (e.g., FlashAttention, PagedAttention) • Support for a wide array of open-source models (Llama, Mistral, Qwen, etc.) • Day-0 support for new model releases
📖 Summary: SGLang is a high-performance serving framework designed for efficient inference of large language models (LLMs) and multimodal models. It offers advanced features like optimized attention mechanisms and supports a wide range of popular models, enabling faster and more scalable AI deployments.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source

🔥 x402 | TypeScript
🎯 Primary Use Case: Enabling seamless, network-agnostic payments over the internet using HTTP.
✨ Key Features: • Open standard for internet-native payments • Supports all networks (crypto & fiat) and forms of value (stablecoins, tokens, fiat) • Built on HTTP, complementing existing data transportation • Network, token, and currency agnostic • Backwards compatible
📖 Summary: x402 is an open standard for internet-native payments, built on HTTP. It aims to support all networks and forms of value, abstracting away complexities like gas fees for clients and resource servers.
🔗 Links: • View Project • Homepage ================ 🔓 Open Source
